behavior_summary: U8P_ALTAR::look handles the object's 'U8P_ALTAR::look' (examine) event: it marks the object with an info/context and a process-exclude to avoid re-processing by the same process, then requests a short descriptive bark saying "altar " directed at the caller/process that triggered the U8P_ALTAR::look.

The routine allocates or converts the literal string "altar ", passes that string and the caller/process pointer to Item::bark, frees the temporary string, performs a small logical/imply operation with the bark return value, then suspends/returns.
notable_control_flow: Entry on the 'U8P_ALTAR::look' event triggers immediate set-info and process-exclude operations (guarding against repeated handling).
Synchronous call to external Item::bark emits the visible description to the invoking process; return value is read and used only in a short implies/pop sequence before suspension.
Temporary string allocation and explicit free occur around the Item::bark call, ensuring no persistent heap allocation remains.
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of the info/context id pushed (inferred from opcode as 0x208) and how it changes runtime behavior beyond 'process exclude'.
Whether the caller/process pointer (BP+06h) is strictly the player/avatar PID or can be other processes in some cases (inferred as viewer).
Any subtle side effects of the implies/logical operation using Item::bark's return value — appears to be discarded, but intent is not explicit.

main_function.name: U8P_ALTAR::look
main_function.purpose: When an actor examines the altar, prepare the altar's info/state to avoid repeated processing and emit a concise description to the examining process by calling Item::bark with the literal "altar ".
entry_conditions: Event 0 — 'U8P_ALTAR::look' (triggered when the Avatar or relevant actor examines/looks at the altar)
Implicit: called with a caller/process pointer (dword at BP+06h) representing the inspecting process

function_map.name: U8P_ALTAR::look
function_map.role: Handle the U8P_ALTAR::look/examine event for the altar, set object info and process-exclude, and request the item's bark string.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit/display a short text message for an item/process (here used to show the altar description 'altar ').

external_calls.purpose: Request that the item produce/display the short description string 'altar ' directed at the calling process; return value is read briefly then ignored.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_ALTAR::look handles the object's 'U8P_ALTAR::look' (examine) event: it marks the object with an info/context and a process-exclude to avoid re-processing by the same process, then requests a short descriptive bark saying "altar " directed at the caller/process that triggered the U8P_ALTAR::look.
